<div class="blog-header">

## [The Deutsch Files II](https://nav.al/deutsch-files-ii)

</div>

<div class="content">

[Brett Hall](https://twitter.com/ToKTeacher) and I interview [David
Deutsch](https://twitter.com/DavidDeutschOxf), physicist and author
of [The Beginning of
Infinity](https://www.google.com/search?q=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&safe=active&client=safari&as_qdr=all&channel=aplab&hl=en&ei=4vXnY87tBvmmqtsP6--duAg&oq=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&gs_lcp=ChNtb2JpbGUtZ3dzLXdpei1zZXJwEANKBAhBGABQ2xdYiyVgwSpoAHAAeAGAAQCIAQCSAQCYAQCgAQHAAQE&sclient=mobile-gws-wiz-serp).
Also see [The Deutsch Files I](http://nav.al/deutsch-files-i). The
universality of computation and explanation So let’s go through [The
Fabric of
Reality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Fabric_of_Reality)—the four
theories.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/deutsch-files-ii" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [The Deutsch Files I](https://nav.al/deutsch-files-i)

</div>

<div class="content">

[Brett Hall](https://twitter.com/ToKTeacher) and I interview [David
Deutsch](https://twitter.com/DavidDeutschOxf), physicist and author
of [The Beginning of
Infinity](https://www.google.com/search?q=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&safe=active&client=safari&as_qdr=all&channel=aplab&hl=en&ei=4vXnY87tBvmmqtsP6--duAg&oq=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&gs_lcp=ChNtb2JpbGUtZ3dzLXdpei1zZXJwEANKBAhBGABQ2xdYiyVgwSpoAHAAeAGAAQCIAQCSAQCYAQCgAQHAAQE&sclient=mobile-gws-wiz-serp). 
New: [Discuss this episode on
Airchat](http://getairchat.com/arjun/deutschfilesdiscussion). We don’t
really have an agenda. There is no goal to the conversation. The closest
we can come up with is just to have a spontaneous free flowing talk
about anything you want to talk
about.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/deutsch-files-i" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [David Deutsch: Knowledge Creation and The Human Race, Part 2](https://nav.al/david-deutsch-2)

</div>

<div class="content">

[Brett Hall](https://twitter.com/ToKTeacher) and I interview [David
Deutsch](https://twitter.com/DavidDeutschOxf?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor),
physicist and author of [The Beginning of
Infinity](https://www.google.com/search?q=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&safe=active&client=safari&as_qdr=all&channel=aplab&hl=en&ei=4vXnY87tBvmmqtsP6--duAg&oq=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&gs_lcp=ChNtb2JpbGUtZ3dzLXdpei1zZXJwEANKBAhBGABQ2xdYiyVgwSpoAHAAeAGAAQCIAQCSAQCYAQCgAQHAAQE&sclient=mobile-gws-wiz-serp).
Also see [Part 1](https://nav.al/david-deutsch). Popper’s Impact One of
the other things that is counterintuitive—and one of the misconceptions
that I see crop up out there in academia, intellectual circles,
education—is that people think that there’s a final
theory.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/david-deutsch-2" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="audio-player-container">

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [David Deutsch: Knowledge Creation and The Human Race, Part 1](https://nav.al/david-deutsch)

</div>

<div class="content">

I interview [David
Deutsch](https://twitter.com/DavidDeutschOxf?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor),
physicist and author of [The Beginning of
Infinity](https://www.google.com/search?q=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&safe=active&client=safari&as_qdr=all&channel=aplab&hl=en&ei=4vXnY87tBvmmqtsP6--duAg&oq=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&gs_lcp=ChNtb2JpbGUtZ3dzLXdpei1zZXJwEANKBAhBGABQ2xdYiyVgwSpoAHAAeAGAAQCIAQCSAQCYAQCgAQHAAQE&sclient=mobile-gws-wiz-serp).
Also see [Part 2](https://nav.al/david-deutsch-2). Background My goal
isn’t to do yet another podcast with David Deutsch. There are plenty of
those. I would love to tease out some of the very counterintuitive
learnings, put them down canonically in such a way that future
generations can benefit from them, and make sure that none of this is
lost.  Your work has been incredibly influential for
me.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/david-deutsch" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="blog-header">

## [Vitalik: Ethereum, Part 2](https://nav.al/vitalik-2)

</div>

<div class="content">

[Haseeb](https://twitter.com/hosseeb) and I interview [Vitalik
Buterin](https://twitter.com/VitalikButerin?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor) about [Ethereum](https://ethereum.org/en/) and
blockchains. Also see [Part 1](https://nav.al/vitalik). Protocol
Politics The elder statesman of smart contract blockchains Vitalik, I
want to ask you a little bit about how your role has evolved since it
began at Ethereum.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/vitalik-2" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Vitalik: Ethereum, Part 1](https://nav.al/vitalik)

</div>

<div class="content">

[Haseeb](https://twitter.com/hosseeb) and I interview [Vitalik
Buterin](https://twitter.com/VitalikButerin?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor)
about [Ethereum](https://ethereum.org/en/) and blockchains. Also see
[Part 2](https://nav.al/vitalik-2). Transcript Welcome back to the
podcast. We have with us [Haseeb
Qureshi](https://twitter.com/hosseeb?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Eauthor),
who is a partner at Dragonfly and someone I used to work with back when
I was more active in
crypto-land.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/vitalik" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="blog-header">

## [The Beginning of Infinity, Part 2](https://nav.al/infinity-2)

</div>

<div class="content">

Part 2 of my interview with [Brett Hall](https://twitter.com/ToKTeacher)
about [The Beginning of
Infinity](https://www.google.com/search?q=The+Beginning+of+Infinity&client=safari&hl=en-us&ei=Vzu-YdCtIYyCwbkPyfa3iAk&ved=0ahUKEwiQtfKnj-70AhUMQTABHUn7DZEQ4dUDCA0&uact=5&oq=The+Beginning+of+Infinity&gs_&sclient=gws-wiz).
Also see [Part 1](https://nav.al/infinity). With a Good Theory of
Knowledge, You Can Decide What Else Is True David Deutsch’s theory is
centered around good explanations [David
Deutsch](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&rls=en&q=david+deutsch&ie=UTF-8&oe=UTF-8) has
this great view of the world where he believes that everything important
is understandable by a single
human.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/infinity-2" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Dec 13 2021

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [To a Caveman Very Few Things Are Resources](https://nav.al/caveman)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

There was a time when coal wasn’t a resource

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

There was a story on ITV in the U.K. talking about how much supposed
waste Amazon produces, that Amazon was routinely destroying a whole
bunch of products. I thought, “Why are these people inserting their
opinion into a business that they know absolutely nothing about?” Would
they prefer Amazon to have the perfect knowledge of precisely how many
products need to be made? In other words, an epistemologically
impossible situation to be
in.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/caveman" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Dec 13 2021

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Dec 10 2021

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Knowledge Makes the Existence of Resources Infinite](https://nav.al/infinite-resources)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

We’re going to keep creating new knowledge and new resources

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Knowledge is the thing that makes the existence of resources infinite.
The creation of knowledge is unbounded. We’re going to keep on creating
more knowledge and, thereby, learning about more and different
resources. There’s this wonderful parable of
[europium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Europium) in The Beginning of
Infinity where David talks about when the first color television started
to be manufactured about 60 years
ago.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/infinite-resources" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

</div>

<div class="date">

Dec 10 2021

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Dec 8 2021

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Groups Never Admit Failure](https://nav.al/failure)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

You get a schism instead

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Groups never admit failure. A group would rather keep living in the
mythology of “we were repressed” than ever admit failure. Individuals
are the only ones who admit failure. Even individuals don’t like to
admit failure, but eventually, they can be forced to. A group will never
admit they were wrong.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/failure" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="controls">
